headline: Agent orchestration loops and free GLM-5.2 tooling push agentic coding to the front of the window.
body: The clearest movement is a shift toward agents that prompt and supervise other agents: engineers behind Claude Code and OpenClaw describe orchestrator-and-worker loops running continuously with database observability rather than manual prompting [11], while Z AI shipped Z Code, a Codex-like coding agent with a free 5 million token per day tier for GLM-5.2 [0]. Workflow automation extends the same theme through Claude Cowork use cases for briefs, triage, and live dashboards [1] and a custom vision agent built on Gemini and the Model Context Protocol [3]. On talent and strategy, Noam Shazeer is joining OpenAI [8][9], Meta's head of product for AI for work is departing [4], and a sovereign AI argument frames vertical integration and full-stack ownership as a strategic imperative [7]. Breakouts skew technical, including continual pretraining, prefill latency, uncertainty quantification, and text-to-speech.

cited_items.title: FULLY FREE GLM-5.2 + Z-Code: This is ACTUALLY GOOD!
